headline: EHS
Cyber
Security
Club
hero.subtitle: Learn ethical hacking, compete in CTFs, and build real-world security skills. Open to all students — no experience required.

about.heading: Why Join the EHS Cyber Club?
about.subheading: Cybersecurity is one of the fastest-growing fields in tech — and you don't need to wait until college to get started. We're a student-led club that makes security skills accessible to everyone, regardless of background.
about.foundedText: Founded in 2019, EHS Cybersecurity Club has grown from a handful of students to one of the largest tech clubs in the district. We meet weekly to learn, practice, and build skills that matter.

pillars.title: Learn
pillars.desc: Hands-on workshops in ethical hacking, malware analysis, and digital forensics taught by students and professionals.

pillars.title: Compete
pillars.desc: Represent EHS in Capture The Flag competitions, CyberPatriot, and national cybersecurity championships.

pillars.title: Build
pillars.desc: Create real security tools, contribute to open source projects, and develop a portfolio that opens doors.

pillars.title: Connect
pillars.desc: Network with industry professionals, mentors, and a community of passionate students who share your interests.

items.title: Our Mission
items.text: To empower every student at EHS with cybersecurity knowledge and skills. Creating the next generation of defenders, builders, and leaders in digital security.

items.title: Our Vision
items.text: A future where every student graduates with the ability to protect themselves and others online. We believe security literacy is as important as reading and writing.

items.title: Our Values
items.text: Inclusivity, ethics, and curiosity above all. We celebrate diversity in thought and background. Hacking is about problem-solving, not breaking things.

cta.heading: Meetings every Thursday at 3:30 PM
cta.subtext: Room 632. Bring a laptop if you have one. No experience needed. Just curiosity.

wwc.heading: Breaking Barriers in Cybersecurity
wwc.subheading: Women are drastically underrepresented in cybersecurity — just 24% of the workforce. We're changing that, starting right here at EHS.

features.title: Safe Space
features.desc: A welcoming environment designed specifically to support women and non-binary students in tech.

features.title: Mentorship
features.desc: Connect with women professionals in cybersecurity, software engineering, and tech leadership.

features.title: Networking
features.desc: Build connections with peers and industry mentors who understand your experience.

features.title: Skill Workshops
features.desc: Coding nights, resume reviews, and interview prep tailored for women entering tech.

quotes.text: "This club showed me that cybersecurity isn't just for guys. I found my community here."
quotes.name: Sarah K., Junior

quotes.text: "The WWC events helped me land my first tech internship. The connections are real."
quotes.name: Maya R., Senior

join.heading: Ready to Join?
join.subheading: Membership is completely free. All skill levels welcome. Whether you're a complete beginner or an experienced hacker, there's a place for you here.

steps.title: Show Up
steps.desc: Come to any Thursday meeting in Room 214 at 3:30 PM. No sign-up required for first visit.

steps.title: Sign the Roster
steps.desc: Fill out our simple membership form. Takes 2 minutes. Gets you on the email list.

steps.title: Start Learning
steps.desc: Jump into workshops, join a CTF team, and connect with the community right away.

join.footerBadge: 100% FREE · NO EXPERIENCE NEEDED · ALL SKILL LEVELS WELCOME

defaultEvents.title: Capture The Flag — Spring 2025
defaultEvents.description: Compete in teams to solve cybersecurity challenges across web exploitation, cryptography, reverse engineering, and forensics.

defaultEvents.title: Intro to Ethical Hacking
defaultEvents.description: Hands-on workshop covering Kali Linux basics, network scanning, and responsible disclosure.

defaultEvents.title: Women Who Code — Networking Night
defaultEvents.description: Connect with women professionals in cybersecurity and tech. Panel discussion, Q&A, and resume review.

defaultEvents.title: Monthly Club Meeting
defaultEvents.description: General meeting: updates, upcoming competition prep, and member showcase.

defaultEvents.title: CyberPatriot Prep Session
defaultEvents.description: Practice rounds for the national CyberPatriot competition. Open to all skill levels.

defaultEvents.title: Women Who Code — Beginner Coding Night
defaultEvents.description: A safe, supportive space to learn Python basics. No experience needed — laptops provided.
